item.title: What happens in California after Gavin Newsom?
item.description: With Gavin Newsom's era as California governor ending, could Tom Steyer be the Democrat to step up? Or will skyrocketing costs and crime finally break the party's grip on California? In this episode of "Make It Make Sense," Carine Hajjar, Jason Willick and James Desio explore the future of the Golden State's political landscape, including whether the GOP can capture the governor's mansion and if Spencer Pratt has a real shot at winning the L.A. mayoral race.

item.title: Trump betrayed farmers. Now real signs of anger show.
item.description: Hurt by the war in Iran and war on trade, will farmers still vote red?

item.title: Why the Metro’s RFK Stadium plan is troubling
item.description: The Commanders are returning to Washington. Will D.C. taxpayers foot the bill?

item.title: The U.S. needs a tough Ebola policy — before the virus gets here
item.description: Covid proved that home quarantine fails. Ebola demands a harder lesson be learned faster.

item.title: The Montana license plate loophole is a warning
item.description: Enforcement alone won’t fix the growing problem. States should address punitive taxes.

item.title: AI fiction is the new fast food
item.description: The world still needs human authors to generate wild ideas.
